routine.title: Public library baby storytime
routine.stage_fit_rationale: Newborns respond to the rhythm and cadence of a parent's voice reading aloud, and a short lap-sit format matches their very limited wake windows without overstimulation. It also aligns directly with the family's interest in books and music through song-along elements typical of baby storytimes.

routine.title: Parent-and-baby drop-in group
routine.stage_fit_rationale: A come-as-you-are drop-in is ideal for the newborn stage because there is no commitment pressure — the family can leave early if the baby is tired or fussy. It provides low-key caregiver social support, which is critical in the early weeks.

parsed.rationale: Two gentle outings anchor the week — a mid-week library visit tied to Hale's book and music interests, and a weekend drop-in for caregiver connection — leaving the rest of the week wide open for feeding, sleeping, and recovery.
parsed.notes: Only two candidates were available, and both are a strong fit for the newborn stage, so no items were left out. Treat both as fully skippable on any given week — newborn schedules shift daily, and missing a session carries zero cost.
